purpose: PreToolUse hook on Edit|Write. Enforces the write-once rule for task IDENTITY in initialization.json. Specifically: blocks Write tool calls that change `task.name` after its initial creation. Edits are always allowed (legitimate use cases: repair task append, dependency rewiring, !add_task — all of which preserve task.name). The first Write to a non-existent file is allowed (initial planning). Subsequent Writes that would set a different task.name are rejected with a message naming both the existing and attempted values. The broader 'fields don't drift' invariants are partly handled by validate-initialization-schema.sh on the Write side and by trust on the Edit side.
gotchas: Edit tool ALWAYS passes — the partial-patch surface is too hard to validate; treat Edit as trusted (repair/add_task path)
Only validates task.name — other identity fields (task.created, task.type) not protected here (schema hook covers them)
Fails open on parse errors — a malformed Write reaches validate-initialization-schema.sh which catches it
Companion hook validate-initialization-schema.sh enforces structural correctness (agents/waves/chains coverage)
patterns: Write-once enforcement via comparing existing-on-disk to new-content from tool_input
Edit/Write asymmetry: Edits trust, Writes validate
